_provenance.note: A small library of KNOWN theorems as grounded predicate-calculus description groups — the 'priors' a novelty detector retrieves against. Functor vocabulary is shared with the targets in grounding/dgroups/ so SME can align a target onto its nearest prior. Authored 2026-06-22; each symbol grounded to a verbatim substring of the theorem statement (checked by grounding/verify.py).

banach_fixed_point.name: banach-fixed-point
banach_fixed_point.text: Let (X,d) be a complete metric space and let T be a self-map on X that is a contraction: there is a constant k in [0,1) such that d(T(x),T(y)) <= k d(x,y) for all x,y in X. Then T has a unique fixed point x_star, and for any starting point x0 the iterates given by x_{n+1} = T(x_n) converge to x_star at a linear rate.

m_estimator_clt.name: m-estimator-clt
m_estimator_clt.text: An M-estimator theta_hat computed from n i.i.d. samples is asymptotically normal: sqrt(n)(theta_hat - theta_star) converges in distribution to a Gaussian N(0, Sigma), where Sigma is the sandwich covariance H^{-1} V H^{-1} with H the Hessian of the population risk and V the covariance of the gradient.

groundings.grad_cov: V the covariance of the gradient

cauchy_schwarz.name: cauchy-schwarz
cauchy_schwarz.text: For vectors u and v in an inner product space, the absolute value of the inner product is bounded by the product of the norms: |<u,v>| <= ||u|| ||v||, with equality if and only if u and v are linearly dependent.
